title: Rebalancing Partitions
summary: When nodes join or leave the cluster, partitions must move — without excessive downtime.
keyTakeaways: Hash mod N breaks when N changes — most keys remap to new nodes.
Consistent hashing minimizes key movement when nodes are added or removed.
Fixed number of partitions with virtual nodes simplifies rebalancing.
Automatic rebalancing risks cascading failures; manual triggers are safer.
Rebalancing should move minimal data at bounded throughput.
Cassandra vnodes, Kafka partition reassignment, and Kubernetes StatefulSet scaling all rebalance data.

body.text: Adding a machine should improve capacity, not invalidate your entire partition map. Naive hash mod N remaps almost every key when N changes. Better schemes use fixed partitions or consistent hashing to limit data movement.

body.title: In practice
body.text: Cassandra uses virtual nodes (vnodes) so adding a host only moves a fraction of data. Kafka's kafka-reassign-partitions tool migrates partitions between brokers with throttling. On Kubernetes, scaling a StatefulSet adds pods but your app still needs a safe rebalance plan — automatic resharding without ops oversight has caused production outages.

body.title: Netflix at scale
body.text: Adding Cassandra nodes during peak streaming hours uses consistent hashing with vnodes so only a fraction of keys move — avoiding the full remap disaster of hash mod N.

body.caption: Consistent hashing on the ring
